id: "13.6.5"
title: "Grafana User Login Audit, Session Anomalies, and Account Takeover Indicators (failed-login spikes, brute force, impossible travel, OAuth token abuse, anonymous org exposure, admin promotion, API key drift, session forgery; OSS Enterprise Cloud SSO SAML OAuth)"

# UC-13.6.5 · Grafana User Login Audit, Session Anomalies, and Account Takeover Indicators (failed-login spikes, brute force, impossible travel, OAuth token abuse, anonymous org exposure, admin promotion, API key drift, session forgery; OSS Enterprise Cloud SSO SAML OAuth)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Picture a headquarters where every dashboard sign-in is a tap at a locked door. We study those taps like a careful guard: too many wrong codes from strangers, impossible jumps between cities faster than an airplane, surprise master keys handed out, or side doors propped open for nameless guests, and we raise the alarm before someone wanders into the control room.*

---

## Description

Detects Grafana authentication abuse and session integrity risks by correlating `logger=auth` outcomes on `grafana:server`, privileged mutations in `grafana:audit`, HTTP semantics on OAuth and token routes in `grafana:http_access`, enterprise IdP sign-in telemetry, and optional edge WAF context so teams surface brute-force clusters, impossible-travel patterns, OAuth token replay pressure, anonymous organization misconfiguration, unexpected administrator promotion, personal access token sprawl, and user-agent anomalies indicative of forged or scripted sessions across self-managed and cloud-hosted Grafana with SAML and OAuth single sign-on.

## Value

Security and platform leaders reduce account takeover mean time to detect when Grafana becomes a high-value pivot because datasource credentials and admin APIs live behind the same browser sessions attackers target, while audit and risk stakeholders receive a joined narrative that ties Splunk findings to owning teams, employment status, and IdP risk signals instead of isolated login noise.

## Implementation

Land grafana:server auth, grafana:audit, grafana:http_access, identity IdP sourcetypes, and optional edge WAF lines; publish grafana_user_inventory.csv and cmdb identity_user_metadata; save security_uc_13_6_5_grafana_login_session_takeover every five to fifteen minutes with earliest twenty-four hours; route critical and high severities to platform security on-call; archive weekly evidence exports to a restricted index.

## Evidence

Saved search security_uc_13_6_5_grafana_login_session_takeover with five-to-fifteen minute schedule; alert routing to platform security on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab Grafana organization, generate twelve failed password attempts for a test user within ten minutes from a single source IP while granting an unexpected administrator role through the UI, then confirm security_uc_13_6_5_grafana_login_session_takeover returns critical severity with non-null recommended_action within two fifteen-minute windows.

### Negative scenario

After restoring normal authentication with MFA-backed SSO exclusively from expected regions and removing test role grants, with inventory showing active employment and no anonymous access enabled, confirm sustained low severity rows only for thirty consecutive scheduled runs when filtering to critical and high.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with identity engineering, observability platform owners who operate Grafana OSS fleets, Grafana Enterprise clusters, and Grafana Cloud organizations, plus the teams that administer corporate single sign-on with Okta, Auth0, Azure Active Directory, or Google Workspace. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.6.2 style data source query failure analytics, not UC-13.6.3 style alert rule evaluation drift, and not UC-13.6.13 style notification delivery tracing. It is the authentication and session integrity story: correlating Grafana server auth logs, Grafana audit records, reverse-proxy HTTP semantics on authentication endpoints, identity provider sign-in telemetry, and optional edge firewall signals so you can see credential abuse, OAuth token replay pressure, SAML group mapping mistakes, anonymous organization misconfiguration, service account and personal access token sprawl, and administrator promotion events before dashboards become a lateral movement beachhead.

Grafana supports many authentication backends and each one changes where evidence appears. LDAP and basic authentication surface prominently in `logger=auth` lines on `grafana:server` with username and password outcomes. GitHub, Google, generic OAuth, Okta, and Azure AD integrations emit OAuth redirects and callback traffic on `/login/oauth/` paths in `grafana:http_access` alongside token refresh behavior you can infer from status codes and latency. SAML and enterprise SSO frequently shift primary failure modes into your identity tenant: group claims that over-provision `GrafanaAdmin`, stale `NameID` mappings after directory hygiene, or clock skew that breaks `NotOnOrAfter` validation. Anonymous access and authentication bypass for specific organizations are powerful for kiosk and demo scenarios yet catastrophic when enabled on production tenants that contain datasource credentials; audit actions that toggle `auth.anonymous` or organization allowlists must be first-class signals. JWT authentication for machine clients introduces replay risk when shared secrets leak or verification relaxes `allowed_audiences`; pair JWT anomalies with API activity rather than treating them as generic web noise.

Session cookie hygiene matters for persistent session forgery narratives. Grafana issues browser cookies after successful login; operators should confirm `cookie_secure`, `cookie_samesite`, and reverse-proxy TLS termination preserve `SameSite` semantics expected by your IdP. When engineers copy live cookies from a browser into scripts, user agent strings often flip from a real browser fingerprint to `curl` or `python-requests`, which this detection uses as a coarse `ua_anomaly` ladder. Cross-region session reuse through consumer VPN exit nodes can look like impossible travel; the heuristic here compares sequential geo estimates with conservative velocity thresholds and should be tuned per workforce mobility patterns.

Grafana role models split between legacy organization roles and fine-grained RBAC in newer releases. Enterprise and Cloud add licensed capabilities yet the audit verbs around permission changes remain comparable: look for grants that elevate contractors to administrator, wildcard folder permissions, or service accounts attached to human identities. Grafana 10 and later emphasizes service accounts over long-lived API keys for automation; migration guides exist because keys bypass interactive MFA and age poorly in configuration repositories. Maintain an inventory CSV keyed on `user_id` with `owner_team`, intended `role`, `mfa_enrolled`, and `employment_status` so joins surface deactivated identities still authenticating.

Audit log destinations include local files, stdout for containers, Grafana Loki, or streaming exports to analytics platforms. Pin retention on audit indexes to exceed your longest incident review horizon and separate dev noise from prod evidence with `env_label` routing derived from host naming. For Grafana Cloud, vendor control planes still emit customer-visible audit categories yet your Splunk ingestion path may differ from self-managed; validate field extractions on representative samples for both. Federation patterns that chain multiple Grafana instances behind auth proxies such as oauth2-proxy or Envoy ext_authz multiply headers and client IP trust boundaries; prefer standardized `X-Forwarded-For` parsing policies and document which hop is authoritative.

Legal and privacy reviewers should approve retention for usernames, IP addresses, and user agents in security indexes. Redact embedded secrets from raw events at ingest. Break-glass accounts belong in a monitored OU with explicit time bounds; correlate their usage with IdP risk events. Replay attack mitigation combines short session lifetimes, rotating signing keys, IdP device posture checks, and step-up MFA on sensitive Grafana actions even when initial login succeeded.

Risk briefing: impossible travel heuristics false alarm for legitimate executive travel and for split-tunnel VPN behaviors. Brute-force thresholds must consider centralized NAT egress from corporate offices. OAuth refresh loops after partial IdP outages can create bursts of HTTP 401 and 302 patterns that resemble attacks until health returns. Tune `severity` ladders with your identity partners and document change windows.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch stanza fans in parallel, plus two knowledge objects for enrichment and one optional edge lane policy.

Lane A, Grafana server authentication: forward file or container logs with `sourcetype=grafana:server` into `index=grafana` or a shared observability index. Confirm `TIME_PREFIX` and `TIME_FORMAT` handle Grafana RFC3339 timestamps. Preserve `logger=auth` lines with outcomes for password, OAuth, and session lifecycle verbs. When Grafana runs on Kubernetes, use an OpenTelemetry Collector `filelog` receiver or a Universal Forwarder sidecar with stable volume mounts; stamp `cluster` and `namespace` fields for UC correlation with `kubernetes_k8s` inventory.

Lane B, Grafana audit: enable Enterprise audit to file or stream, or equivalent Cloud audit exports, landing as `sourcetype=grafana:audit` with JSON or key-value parsing that extracts `action`, `username`, `orgId`, and resource identifiers. Include permission changes, role assignments, API token creation, and organization setting mutations. If you multiplex audit with other Grafana JSON, isolate `action` families with transforms before this search executes.

Lane C, Identity provider sign-ins: ingest Okta System Log and Azure AD sign-in risk feeds into `index=identity` with sourcetypes such as `okta:system` and `azure_ad:signin`. Normalize a shared `user_id` lowercase key and client IP fields. Map failure reason codes into a compact `outcome` string for correlation with Grafana `is_fail` flags. For Auth0 or Google Workspace, mirror the same schema with add-on field aliases so joins remain stable.

Lane D, Grafana HTTP access for auth endpoints: capture `sourcetype=grafana:http_access` from nginx, Envoy, HAProxy, or cloud load balancers in front of Grafana. Ensure URI paths retain `/api/auth/`, `/api/user/auth-tokens`, `/login/oauth/`, and `/login` prefixes. Forward client IP and user agent faithfully; if you terminate TLS at a service mesh, preserve the original source on a trusted header only after anti-spoofing controls.

Lane E, edge WAF optional arm: land `sourcetype=cloudflare:waf` or equivalent in `index=edge` for IP reputation and bot scores on Grafana hostnames. This arm raises `ua_anomaly` context when scanners hammer login routes without ever completing OAuth.

Knowledge object one: maintain `lookups/grafana_user_inventory.csv` with columns `user_id`, `owner_team`, `role`, `mfa_enrolled`, `employment_status`, and `runbook_url`. Refresh from HR systems and Grafana admin API exports under change control.

Knowledge object two: maintain `index=cmdb` with `sourcetype=identity_user_metadata` carrying `contractor_flag`, `identity_source`, and `last_hr_sync` for left joins on `user_id`.

Example curl helpers from an administrative bastion after authenticating with a scoped service token:

```bash
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" https://grafana.example.org/api/org/users | head
curl -fsS https://grafana.example.org/api/org/preferences | jq .
```

Pre-save validation: run one-hour windows confirming each sourcetype produces events, that a failed password attempt in lab increments `is_fail`, and that an intentional permission grant appears in `grafana:audit` with non-zero `admin_promotion_event` in the pipeline.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as `security_uc_13_6_5_grafana_login_session_takeover` with a five or fifteen minute schedule in production, `dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and alert when `severity` is `critical` or `high`. Throttle duplicate `user_id` and `ip` pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include `recommended_action`, `failed_login_count_15min`, and `impossible_travel_velocity_kmh` in pager narratives so incident responders open IdP and Grafana runbooks in parallel.

Pipeline narrative: `multisearch` fans Grafana auth server lines, Grafana audit mutations, IdP sign-ins, Grafana HTTP auth paths, and optional WAF reputation rows so a silent single lane cannot hide account takeover precursors. `coalesce()` absorbs field naming drift across OSS, Enterprise, and Cloud samples. `streamstats` constructs sliding fifteen minute success and failure counts per user, twenty-four hour distinct IP counts per user, and rolling maxima for permission and anonymous-enable flags. `eventstats` adds per-environment baselines for failed login density to support future adaptive thresholds in mature deployments. `case()` ladders encode critical conditions for brute force density, extreme travel velocity, administrator promotion, and anonymous organization exposure, then map residual patterns into high, medium, and low tiers. `inputlookup` joins inventory ownership and MFA enrollment while a subsearch `join` enriches HR metadata. The closing `table` presents investigator-ready columns aligned to authentication narratives.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level `spl` JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.5 Grafana login audit session takeover correlation auth grafana:server grafana:audit IdP HTTP OAuth multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:server logger=auth earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lm,"login|password|auth|oauth|session|credential")
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,user_id,username,login,email,user,""))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ip,remote_addr,ClientHost,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(user_agent,http_user_agent,UserAgent,ua,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval is_fail=if(match(lm,"invalid|failed|denied|unauthor|incorrect|401|403|bad password|locked"),1,0)
      | eval is_ok=if(is_fail==0 AND match(lm,"logged in|login succeeded|authenticated|success|oauth ok|user logged"),1,0)
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=0
      | eval lane="auth_server" ]
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,""))))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,user_id,username,actor,email,uname,""))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ip,remote_addr,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(user_agent,http_user_agent,ua,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval admin_promotion_event=if((match(act,"permission|role|admin|grafana_admin|users.role") OR match(lr,"permission|role|admin")) AND match(lr,"add|grant|promote|assign|update|set"),1,0)
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=if(match(lr,"anonymous|auth.anonymous|enable anonymous|allow anonymous"),1,0)
      | eval api_key_inc=if(match(act,"api.key|pat|service.token") OR match(lr,"api key|auth token|personal access token|create token"),1,0)
      | eval is_fail=0
      | eval is_ok=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=if(match(ua,"curl|python-requests|scanner|nikto|sqlmap|go-http-client"),3,0)
      | eval lane="grafana_audit" ]
    [ search index=identity (sourcetype=okta:system OR sourcetype=azure_ad:signin) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(toString(coalesce(_raw,"")))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,user_principal_name,UserPrincipalName,alternateId,""))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ipAddress,signInIPAddress,ClientIP,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(userAgent,UserAgent,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,resourceGroup,"idp")))
      | eval oc=lower(toString(coalesce(outcome.result,result,status,outcome,"success")))
      | eval is_fail=if(match(oc,"fail|denied|error|blocked|interrupted"),1,0)
      | eval is_ok=if(is_fail==0 AND len(user_id)>2,1,0)
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=if(match(ua,"curl|python|scanner"),2,0)
      | eval lane="idp_signin" ]
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:http_access earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/api/auth/%") OR like(uri,"%/api/user/auth-tokens%") OR like(uri,"%/login/oauth/%") OR like(uri,"%/login%")
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(remote_user,username,user,userid,"http_sess"))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,remote_addr,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(http_user_agent,user_agent,ua,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,"0")))
      | eval st=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(status,http_status,status_code,"200"))),10)
      | eval is_fail=if(st>=400,1,0)
      | eval is_ok=if(st>=200 AND st<300,1,0)
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=if(like(uri,"%auth-tokens%") AND is_ok==1,1,0)
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=if(match(ua,"curl|python-requests|go-http|java/"),2,0)
      | eval lane="http_auth_api" ]
    [ search index=edge sourcetype=cloudflare:waf earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(clientIP,ClientIP,src,"")))
      | eval user_id="edge_reputation_actor"
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(userAgent,"")))
      | eval org_id="edge"
      | eval is_fail=1
      | eval is_ok=0
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=4
      | eval lane="edge_waf" ]
| eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,email,login,"unknown_user"))))
| eval ip=coalesce(nullif(trim(ip),""),"0.0.0.0")
| eval ua=coalesce(nullif(trim(ua),""),"unknown_ua")
| eval org_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(org_id),""),"0")
| iplocation ip
| eval geo=trim(toString(coalesce(Country,Region,city,CountryCode,"unknown_geo")))
| eval env_label=case(match(host,"dev|sandbox|lab"),"nonprod", true(),"prod")
| sort 0 user_id _time
| streamstats time_window=900 window=50000 current=t global=f sum(is_fail) AS failed_login_count_15min sum(is_ok) AS successful_login_count_15min BY user_id
| streamstats time_window=86400 window=50000 current=t global=f dc(ip) AS distinct_ip_count_24h BY user_id
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f first(_time) AS prev_time first(lat) AS prev_lat first(lon) AS prev_lon BY user_id
| eval dt=if(isnotnull(prev_time),_time-prev_time,0)
| eval dist_km=if(isnotnull(prev_lat) AND isnotnull(lat) AND isnotnull(prev_lon) AND isnotnull(lon) AND dt>0, sqrt(pow(69.1*(lat-prev_lat),2)+pow(69.1*(lon-prev_lon)*cos(lat*3.14159265/180),2))*1.60934, 0)
| eval impossible_travel_velocity_kmh=if(dt>60 AND dist_km>50, round((dist_km/dt)*3600,2), 0)
| streamstats window=200 current=t global=f max(admin_promotion_event) AS admin_promotion_event BY user_id
| streamstats window=200 current=t global=f max(anonymous_org_enabled) AS anonymous_org_enabled BY user_id
| streamstats time_window=86400 window=50000 current=t global=f sum(api_key_inc) AS api_key_create_count BY user_id
| streamstats window=50 current=t global=f max(ua_anomaly_score) AS ua_anomaly BY user_id
| eventstats avg(failed_login_count_15min) AS env_fail_baseline stdev(failed_login_count_15min) AS env_fail_std BY env_label
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=10 OR coalesce(impossible_travel_velocity_kmh,0)>800 OR coalesce(admin_promotion_event,0)>=1 OR coalesce(anonymous_org_enabled,0)>=1, "critical",
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=5 OR coalesce(distinct_ip_count_24h,0)>=8 OR coalesce(ua_anomaly,0)>=3 OR coalesce(api_key_create_count,0)>=3, "high",
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=3 OR coalesce(impossible_travel_velocity_kmh,0)>400, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(anonymous_org_enabled,0)>=1, "disable_anonymous_org_review_auth_settings_and_org_allowlist",
    coalesce(admin_promotion_event,0)>=1, "revoke_unexpected_role_changes_validate_saml_group_maps_and_breakglass",
    coalesce(impossible_travel_velocity_kmh,0)>800, "force_step_up_mfa_invalidate_sessions_review_idp_risk_signals",
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=10, "block_src_ip_rate_limit_login_review_password_spray_and_captcha_policy",
    coalesce(api_key_create_count,0)>=3, "audit_pat_service_accounts_rotate_leaked_keys_enforce_sa_migration",
    coalesce(ua_anomaly,0)>=3, "inspect_automation_scanning_paths_waf_tuning_and_oauth_redirect_allowlist",
    true(), "correlate_idp_signin_grafana_http_and_audit_for_session_replay")
| join type=left max=0 user_id
    [| inputlookup grafana_user_inventory.csv
     | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,userid,login,""))))
     | fields user_id owner_team role mfa_enrolled employment_status runbook_url]
| join type=left max=0 user_id
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=identity_user_metadata earliest=-7d latest=now
     | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,userid,login,""))))
     | dedup user_id
     | fields user_id identity_source contractor_flag last_hr_sync]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval employment_status=coalesce(employment_status,contractor_flag,"active_assumed")
| eval role=coalesce(role,"unknown")
| table _time user_id owner_team employment_status role ip geo org_id failed_login_count_15min successful_login_count_15min distinct_ip_count_24h impossible_travel_velocity_kmh admin_promotion_event severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[security_uc_13_6_5_grafana_login_session_takeover_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-security-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana auth risk $result.severity$ user=$result.user_id$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch security_uc_13_6_5_grafana_login_session_takeover | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal targets, materialize each lane hourly into a summary index keyed on `user_id` and `org_id`, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large Grafana Cloud estates, split searches by `org_id` using a macro token. Cap WAF arm volume with sampling only after security architecture approves the blind spot.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Grafana authentication settings and the identity provider admin console for the same window. Reproduce a failed password attempt in lab and confirm `grafana:server` `logger=auth` lines appear in Splunk with matching `user_id` and `ip` within one collection interval. Grant a test permission change and verify `grafana:audit` drives `admin_promotion_event` non-zero for that actor.

Cross-check IdP narratives: for each `critical` row, open Okta or Azure AD views for the user showing concurrent sessions, unfamiliar locations, or risky sign-ins. Compare `grafana:http_access` status codes on `/login/oauth/` with IdP health dashboards during suspected OAuth refresh loops.

Travel heuristic validation: pick two sequential events flagged with high velocity and confirm timestamps and geo estimates are trustworthy; discard obvious VPN false positives by enriching with corporate egress allowlists in a follow-on lookup.

Synthetic positive test: run scripted failed logins below production lockout thresholds against a sacrificial test user and confirm `failed_login_count_15min` crosses `high` or `critical` within two windows. Synthetic negative test: resume normal logins exclusively from a single metro area and confirm sustained `low` severity for thirty consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document expected spikes during quarterly penetration tests and red team exercises so validators compare against approved attack calendars rather than filing defects.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Long-tail brute force from rotating residential IPs: correlate WAF and `grafana:http_access` bursts, enable progressive CAPTCHA and rate limits at the edge, and require MFA for all Grafana orgs with datasource secrets.

Case 2 — Legitimate engineer travel mis-flagged as impossible travel: validate VPN and corporate travel calendars, widen velocity thresholds for road-warrior personas, and enrich with IdP device compliance signals before paging.

Case 3 — OAuth refresh-token loop after partial IdP outage: replay HTTP status timelines, restore IdP availability, invalidate stale Grafana sessions, and confirm Grafana OAuth client secrets match vendor console entries.

Case 4 — Administrator accidentally enabled anonymous organization access during a demo: revert `auth.anonymous` settings, audit datasource credentials exposed to viewers, and require executive sign-off for any future kiosk mode.

Case 5 — Misconfigured SAML group claim promotes contractor to GrafanaAdmin: fix attribute mappings in the enterprise IdP, revoke over-provisioned roles, and add approval workflows for federation changes.

Case 6 — Leaked API key reused after rotation skip: hunt for continued `auth-tokens` success from unfamiliar automation user agents, delete orphaned keys, migrate callers to scoped service accounts with short TTLs.

Case 7 — Bot scanner targets `/login` without completing OAuth: tune WAF rules, block scanner ASNs temporarily, and confirm Grafana `root_url` and redirect URIs cannot be abused for open redirects.

Case 8 — Failed-login spike during planned IdP migration: gate alerts with change metadata, compare against maintenance tickets, and temporarily raise thresholds only with identity leadership approval.

Case 9 — Over-permissioned service account performing human-like browsing: separate automation identities from interactive SSO, enforce RBAC least privilege, and alert on service accounts attaching to browser user agents.

Case 10 — User re-using browser cookie after HR deactivation: confirm IdP session termination hooks, revoke Grafana sessions server-side, and validate inventory `employment_status` joins suppress residual access.

Case 11 — Cross-region session reuse via consumer VPN exit nodes: treat geo anomalies as hints not verdicts, pivot on AS number stability and device cookies, and coordinate with insider risk processes.

Case 12 — Abandoned API keys older than one hundred eighty days suddenly used: enforce key expiration policies, archive Grafana audit exports showing creation actors, and require quarterly key hygiene attestation from owning teams.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the `spl` field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; required SPL verbs appear; final `table` includes fifteen investigator columns; inventory and CMDB joins provide ownership and employment context.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.5 Grafana login audit session takeover correlation auth grafana:server grafana:audit IdP HTTP OAuth multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:server logger=auth earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lm,"login|password|auth|oauth|session|credential")
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,user_id,username,login,email,user,""))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ip,remote_addr,ClientHost,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(user_agent,http_user_agent,UserAgent,ua,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval is_fail=if(match(lm,"invalid|failed|denied|unauthor|incorrect|401|403|bad password|locked"),1,0)
      | eval is_ok=if(is_fail==0 AND match(lm,"logged in|login succeeded|authenticated|success|oauth ok|user logged"),1,0)
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=0
      | eval lane="auth_server" ]
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval act=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,""))))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,user_id,username,actor,email,uname,""))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ip,remote_addr,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(user_agent,http_user_agent,ua,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval admin_promotion_event=if((match(act,"permission|role|admin|grafana_admin|users.role") OR match(lr,"permission|role|admin")) AND match(lr,"add|grant|promote|assign|update|set"),1,0)
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=if(match(lr,"anonymous|auth.anonymous|enable anonymous|allow anonymous"),1,0)
      | eval api_key_inc=if(match(act,"api.key|pat|service.token") OR match(lr,"api key|auth token|personal access token|create token"),1,0)
      | eval is_fail=0
      | eval is_ok=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=if(match(ua,"curl|python-requests|scanner|nikto|sqlmap|go-http-client"),3,0)
      | eval lane="grafana_audit" ]
    [ search index=identity (sourcetype=okta:system OR sourcetype=azure_ad:signin) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(toString(coalesce(_raw,"")))
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user,user_principal_name,UserPrincipalName,alternateId,""))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ipAddress,signInIPAddress,ClientIP,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(userAgent,UserAgent,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,resourceGroup,"idp")))
      | eval oc=lower(toString(coalesce(outcome.result,result,status,outcome,"success")))
      | eval is_fail=if(match(oc,"fail|denied|error|blocked|interrupted"),1,0)
      | eval is_ok=if(is_fail==0 AND len(user_id)>2,1,0)
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=if(match(ua,"curl|python|scanner"),2,0)
      | eval lane="idp_signin" ]
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:http_access earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/api/auth/%") OR like(uri,"%/api/user/auth-tokens%") OR like(uri,"%/login/oauth/%") OR like(uri,"%/login%")
      | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(remote_user,username,user,userid,"http_sess"))))
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,remote_addr,"")))
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(http_user_agent,user_agent,ua,"")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,"0")))
      | eval st=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(status,http_status,status_code,"200"))),10)
      | eval is_fail=if(st>=400,1,0)
      | eval is_ok=if(st>=200 AND st<300,1,0)
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=if(like(uri,"%auth-tokens%") AND is_ok==1,1,0)
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=if(match(ua,"curl|python-requests|go-http|java/"),2,0)
      | eval lane="http_auth_api" ]
    [ search index=edge sourcetype=cloudflare:waf earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval ip=trim(toString(coalesce(clientIP,ClientIP,src,"")))
      | eval user_id="edge_reputation_actor"
      | eval ua=trim(toString(coalesce(userAgent,"")))
      | eval org_id="edge"
      | eval is_fail=1
      | eval is_ok=0
      | eval admin_promotion_event=0
      | eval anonymous_org_enabled=0
      | eval api_key_inc=0
      | eval ua_anomaly_score=4
      | eval lane="edge_waf" ]
| eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,email,login,"unknown_user"))))
| eval ip=coalesce(nullif(trim(ip),""),"0.0.0.0")
| eval ua=coalesce(nullif(trim(ua),""),"unknown_ua")
| eval org_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(org_id),""),"0")
| iplocation ip
| eval geo=trim(toString(coalesce(Country,Region,city,CountryCode,"unknown_geo")))
| eval env_label=case(match(host,"dev|sandbox|lab"),"nonprod", true(),"prod")
| sort 0 user_id _time
| streamstats time_window=900 window=50000 current=t global=f sum(is_fail) AS failed_login_count_15min sum(is_ok) AS successful_login_count_15min BY user_id
| streamstats time_window=86400 window=50000 current=t global=f dc(ip) AS distinct_ip_count_24h BY user_id
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f first(_time) AS prev_time first(lat) AS prev_lat first(lon) AS prev_lon BY user_id
| eval dt=if(isnotnull(prev_time),_time-prev_time,0)
| eval dist_km=if(isnotnull(prev_lat) AND isnotnull(lat) AND isnotnull(prev_lon) AND isnotnull(lon) AND dt>0, sqrt(pow(69.1*(lat-prev_lat),2)+pow(69.1*(lon-prev_lon)*cos(lat*3.14159265/180),2))*1.60934, 0)
| eval impossible_travel_velocity_kmh=if(dt>60 AND dist_km>50, round((dist_km/dt)*3600,2), 0)
| streamstats window=200 current=t global=f max(admin_promotion_event) AS admin_promotion_event BY user_id
| streamstats window=200 current=t global=f max(anonymous_org_enabled) AS anonymous_org_enabled BY user_id
| streamstats time_window=86400 window=50000 current=t global=f sum(api_key_inc) AS api_key_create_count BY user_id
| streamstats window=50 current=t global=f max(ua_anomaly_score) AS ua_anomaly BY user_id
| eventstats avg(failed_login_count_15min) AS env_fail_baseline stdev(failed_login_count_15min) AS env_fail_std BY env_label
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=10 OR coalesce(impossible_travel_velocity_kmh,0)>800 OR coalesce(admin_promotion_event,0)>=1 OR coalesce(anonymous_org_enabled,0)>=1, "critical",
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=5 OR coalesce(distinct_ip_count_24h,0)>=8 OR coalesce(ua_anomaly,0)>=3 OR coalesce(api_key_create_count,0)>=3, "high",
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=3 OR coalesce(impossible_travel_velocity_kmh,0)>400, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(anonymous_org_enabled,0)>=1, "disable_anonymous_org_review_auth_settings_and_org_allowlist",
    coalesce(admin_promotion_event,0)>=1, "revoke_unexpected_role_changes_validate_saml_group_maps_and_breakglass",
    coalesce(impossible_travel_velocity_kmh,0)>800, "force_step_up_mfa_invalidate_sessions_review_idp_risk_signals",
    coalesce(failed_login_count_15min,0)>=10, "block_src_ip_rate_limit_login_review_password_spray_and_captcha_policy",
    coalesce(api_key_create_count,0)>=3, "audit_pat_service_accounts_rotate_leaked_keys_enforce_sa_migration",
    coalesce(ua_anomaly,0)>=3, "inspect_automation_scanning_paths_waf_tuning_and_oauth_redirect_allowlist",
    true(), "correlate_idp_signin_grafana_http_and_audit_for_session_replay")
| join type=left max=0 user_id
    [| inputlookup grafana_user_inventory.csv
     | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,userid,login,""))))
     | fields user_id owner_team role mfa_enrolled employment_status runbook_url]
| join type=left max=0 user_id
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=identity_user_metadata earliest=-7d latest=now
     | eval user_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user_id,userid,login,""))))
     | dedup user_id
     | fields user_id identity_source contractor_flag last_hr_sync]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval employment_status=coalesce(employment_status,contractor_flag,"active_assumed")
| eval role=coalesce(role,"unknown")
| table _time user_id owner_team employment_status role ip geo org_id failed_login_count_15min successful_login_count_15min distinct_ip_count_24h impossible_travel_velocity_kmh admin_promotion_event severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Authentication.user) AS user latest(Authentication.src) AS src latest(Authentication.action) AS action FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication Authentication.app="grafana" OR Authentication.dest="grafana" earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.user
| rename Authentication.user AS user_id Authentication.src AS ip
| join type=left max=0 user_id [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Change.object) AS chg_object latest(Change.action) AS chg_action FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change Change.object="grafana_rbac" earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.user
| rename Change.user AS user_id]
| table user_id ip action chg_object chg_action
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by user_id and org_id with drilldowns to raw grafana:server, grafana:audit, grafana:http_access, and IdP events; map geo hints for travel anomalies; single-value tiles for failed_login_count_15min and distinct_ip_count_24h.

## Known False Positives

Scheduled identity provider cutovers and certificate rotations produce short bursts of failed Grafana logins and OAuth callbacks that resemble credential stuffing until traffic stabilizes; correlate with change tickets before declaring an incident. Approved change windows that temporarily enable anonymous access for customer demos in non-production tenants will trip anonymous-org signals unless lookup metadata marks those org identifiers as exempt. Contractor onboarding waves may spike legitimate first-time logins from new countries and ASNs; require HR join confirmation before treating geo anomalies as takeover. Cloud egress NAT pools can concentrate many benign users behind one public IP yet trigger IP-reputation alarms at the edge; maintain corporate egress allowlists and tune WAF scoring. Automated test suites that authenticate repeatedly to Grafana for synthetic monitoring inflate failure counters unless synthetic users are labeled in inventory and excluded with explicit policy. Red-team exercises and password-spray simulations will cross brute-force thresholds by design; mute only with named exercise metadata and executive approval. Split-tunnel VPN clients may emit sequential geo estimates that exaggerate travel velocity for road warriors; pair signals with device compliance and step-up outcomes. Service accounts that use headless HTTP clients share user agent strings with attackers; downgrade purely UA-based escalations when automation ownership is documented.

- [OAuth2 Proxy — Documentation](https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy/)
- [Grafana — Configure SAML authentication](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-security/configure-authentication/saml/)
- [Grafana — HTTP API (authentication and tokens)](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/developers/http_api/authentication/)
- [Grafana — Service accounts](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/administration/service-accounts/)
- [OWASP — Authentication Cheat Sheet](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Authentication_Cheat_Sheet.html)
- [NIST SP 800-63B — Digital Identity Guidelines](https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-3/sp800-63b.html)
